suboptimal_algorithms.name: Naive O(n²) nested loop scan
suboptimal_algorithms.how_common: most common brute force — student scans all pairs

suboptimal_algorithms.name: Sorting-based O(n log n) when O(n) is expected
suboptimal_algorithms.how_common: student sorts instead of using counting or hashing

large_adversarial.name: v_shape
large_adversarial.construction_algorithm:
mid = N // 2
arr = list(range(mid, 0, -1)) + list(range(1, mid + 1))
large_adversarial.why_works: V-shape maximizes the number of comparisons needed when scanning for min/max pairs. Naive O(n²) scans all pairs.
large_adversarial.optimal_safe: O(n) single-pass solutions process each element once regardless of shape.

large_adversarial.name: alternating_max_min
large_adversarial.construction_algorithm:
arr = []
lo, hi = 1, 10**9
for i in range(N):
    arr.append(lo if i % 2 == 0 else hi)
    lo += 1; hi -= 1
large_adversarial.why_works: Maximum distance between adjacent elements forces worst-case for any algorithm that tracks local relationships.
large_adversarial.optimal_safe: Hash-based or prefix-based O(n) solutions unaffected by value distribution.

edge_cases.name: single_element
edge_cases.bug_caught: off-by-one in loop bounds, arr[i+1] access when N=1

edge_cases.name: two_elements
edge_cases.bug_caught: minimal swap logic

edge_cases.name: all_identical
edge_cases.bug_caught: duplicate handling, early termination assumptions

edge_cases.name: strictly_increasing
edge_cases.bug_caught: already-answer cases, unnecessary processing

edge_cases.name: strictly_decreasing
edge_cases.bug_caught: maximum work for most naive algorithms

suboptimal_algorithms.name: Naive QuickSort (first-element pivot)
suboptimal_algorithms.how_common: most common student implementation

suboptimal_algorithms.name: Bubble / Selection / Insertion Sort
suboptimal_algorithms.how_common: beginner students

large_adversarial.name: reverse_sorted
large_adversarial.construction_algorithm: arr = list(range(N, 0, -1))
large_adversarial.why_works: First element is always max, creating 0 vs N-1 partition splits every level. Forces O(n²) comparisons.
large_adversarial.optimal_safe: Merge sort splits at midpoint regardless. Python sorted() uses Timsort which detects descending runs.

large_adversarial.name: median_of_three_killer
large_adversarial.construction_algorithm:
candidate = list(range(N))
killer = [0] * N
for i in range(N // 2):
    killer[2 * i] = candidate[i]
    killer[2 * i + 1] = candidate[N - 1 - i]
return killer
large_adversarial.why_works: Forces median-of-three pivot to always select near-min/max, causing maximally unbalanced partitions.
large_adversarial.optimal_safe: Introsort (C++ std::sort) detects depth limit and switches to heapsort. Merge sort unaffected.

edge_cases.name: already_sorted
edge_cases.bug_caught: first-element-pivot QuickSort O(n²) on sorted input

edge_cases.name: reverse_sorted
edge_cases.bug_caught: first-element-pivot QuickSort O(n²) on reverse input

edge_cases.name: all_identical
edge_cases.bug_caught: duplicate handling in partition, three-way partition assumption

edge_cases.name: two_unique_values
edge_cases.construction_algorithm: arr = [1 if i % 2 == 0 else 2 for i in range(N)]
edge_cases.bug_caught: unstable sort behavior with equal elements

edge_cases.name: single_element
edge_cases.bug_caught: loop bounds arr[i+1] access when n=1

suboptimal_algorithms.name: Naive O(N*M) substring search
suboptimal_algorithms.how_common: students who don't know KMP or Z-function

large_adversarial.name: fibonacci_string
large_adversarial.construction_algorithm:
def fib_str(k):
    a, b = 'a', 'b'
    for _ in range(k - 2):
        a, b = b, b + a
    return b
text = fib_str(k + 4)
pattern = fib_str(k)
large_adversarial.why_works: Highly periodic with no clean period. Naive search restarts at every mismatch causing O(NM) total comparisons.
large_adversarial.optimal_safe: KMP failure function handles periodic patterns in O(N+M).

large_adversarial.name: all_same_char_late_mismatch
large_adversarial.construction_algorithm:
text = 'a' * (N - 1) + 'b'
pattern = 'a' * (M - 1) + 'b'
large_adversarial.why_works: Every alignment: M-1 successes then 1 failure. Total: O(N*M) comparisons.
large_adversarial.optimal_safe: KMP skips matched prefix on mismatch using failure function.

edge_cases.name: pattern_equals_text
edge_cases.bug_caught: match at index 0, boundary handling

edge_cases.name: pattern_longer_than_text
edge_cases.bug_caught: missing early-exit condition

edge_cases.name: no_occurrence
edge_cases.construction_algorithm: text has no occurrence of pattern
edge_cases.bug_caught: returning garbage instead of -1 or empty

edge_cases.name: overlapping_matches
edge_cases.bug_caught: advancing by len(pattern) instead of 1 after match

suboptimal_algorithms.name: Linear scan for each element O(n²)
suboptimal_algorithms.how_common: students who re-scan for next greater/smaller without stack

large_adversarial.name: sawtooth_pattern
large_adversarial.construction_algorithm:
arr = []
for i in range(N // 2):
    arr.append(i + 1)
    arr.append(N - i)
large_adversarial.why_works: Alternating low-high forces maximum push/pop operations per element for naive approaches.
large_adversarial.optimal_safe: Monotonic stack processes each element at most twice total regardless of pattern.

large_adversarial.name: increasing_then_spike
large_adversarial.construction_algorithm: arr = list(range(1, N)) + [10 ** 9]
large_adversarial.why_works: All N-1 elements wait for the spike at the end. Naive scan: (N-1)² comparisons. Stack: 2N operations.
large_adversarial.optimal_safe: Monotonic stack clears in O(N) at the spike.

edge_cases.name: empty_stack_pop
edge_cases.construction_algorithm: Input that would cause an empty stack pop if not handled: single element
edge_cases.bug_caught: stack[-1] on empty stack

edge_cases.name: all_identical
edge_cases.bug_caught: strict vs non-strict comparison in stack condition

edge_cases.name: strictly_decreasing
edge_cases.bug_caught: elements that are never popped — all remain in stack at end

edge_cases.name: strictly_increasing
edge_cases.bug_caught: each element immediately pops previous — maximum pop operations

suboptimal_algorithms.name: list.pop(0) instead of deque.popleft()
suboptimal_algorithms.complexity: O(n) per dequeue instead of O(1)
suboptimal_algorithms.how_common: students using list as queue

large_adversarial.name: rapid_enqueue_dequeue
large_adversarial.construction_algorithm:
# N alternating insert/remove operations
ops = []
for i in range(N // 2):
    ops.append(('enqueue', i))
    ops.append(('dequeue',))
large_adversarial.why_works: Each list.pop(0) shifts all remaining elements. N/2 dequeues × N/2 remaining = O(n²). Deque is O(1) amortized.
large_adversarial.optimal_safe: collections.deque.popleft() is O(1). Total: O(n).

edge_cases.name: dequeue_empty
edge_cases.bug_caught: missing empty check before pop

edge_cases.name: single_element_enqueue_dequeue
edge_cases.bug_caught: size tracking when queue goes 0→1→0

suboptimal_algorithms.name: Sorting entire array for each extraction
suboptimal_algorithms.how_common: students who sort instead of using heap

large_adversarial.name: interleaved_insert_extract
large_adversarial.why_works: Re-sorting after each insert is O(N log N). Total: O(N² log N). Heap maintains order in O(log N) per op.
large_adversarial.optimal_safe: Heap insert/extract both O(log N). N operations = O(N log N) total.

edge_cases.name: already_sorted_ascending
edge_cases.bug_caught: heapify efficiency, sift-down already minimal

suboptimal_algorithms.name: Naive recursion without memoization

suboptimal_algorithms.name: Wrong state definition leading to O(N³) instead of O(N²)
suboptimal_algorithms.how_common: intermediate students with subtle state bugs

large_adversarial.name: maximum_overlapping_subproblems
large_adversarial.construction_algorithm:
# For LCS: two identical strings
s1 = 'a' * N
s2 = 'a' * N
# For knapsack: items with weight[i]=1, value[i]=1, capacity=N//2
# For coin change: coins=[1,2], target=N
large_adversarial.why_works: Every subproblem is recomputed exponentially many times without memoization.
large_adversarial.optimal_safe: Bottom-up DP computes each state exactly once. Total states × transition cost.

suboptimal_algorithms.name: Recursive DFS without stack size increase
suboptimal_algorithms.how_common: students who know DFS but not iterative version

suboptimal_algorithms.name: Adjacency matrix O(V²) scan
suboptimal_algorithms.how_common: students using matrix instead of adjacency list

large_adversarial.name: bamboo_chain
large_adversarial.construction_algorithm: edges = [(i, i + 1) for i in range(1, N)]
large_adversarial.why_works: Recursion depth equals N. Python default limit is 1000. Stack memory exhausts at N=10^5.
large_adversarial.optimal_safe: Iterative BFS/DFS uses explicit stack, O(V+E), unaffected by depth.

large_adversarial.name: complete_graph
large_adversarial.construction_algorithm:
N = 3000
edges = [(i, j) for i in range(1, N + 1) for j in range(i + 1, N + 1)]
large_adversarial.why_works: O(V²) matrix scan: 3000² = 9M ops. Adjacency list BFS: O(V+E) same complexity, smaller constant.
large_adversarial.optimal_safe: Adjacency list processes each edge once in BFS. Total work O(V+E).

edge_cases.name: single_node_no_edges
edge_cases.bug_caught: empty adjacency list handling, loop on zero neighbors

edge_cases.name: disconnected_components
edge_cases.bug_caught: assuming graph is connected, not iterating all components

edge_cases.name: self_loop
edge_cases.bug_caught: visited check timing — before or after enqueue

edge_cases.name: star_graph
edge_cases.construction_algorithm: edges = [(1, i) for i in range(2, N + 1)]
edge_cases.bug_caught: degree handling, node 1 has N-1 neighbors

O(V+E).reasoning: Standard BFS/DFS handles 10^5 nodes and 2×10^5 edges comfortably in 2s

suboptimal_algorithms.name: O(N) trial division for each query
suboptimal_algorithms.how_common: students checking all divisors up to N instead of sqrt(N)

suboptimal_algorithms.name: No sieve — repeated primality checks
suboptimal_algorithms.how_common: students who don't precompute primes

large_adversarial.name: large_primes_near_limit
large_adversarial.construction_algorithm:
# Use large primes just below constraint limit
# For primality: values like 999999937 (prime near 10^9)
# Q queries each checking a large prime
values = [999999937] * Q
large_adversarial.why_works: O(N) check on N=10^9: 10^9 operations per query × Q queries → TLE. O(sqrt(N)): 31623 ops per query → fast.
large_adversarial.optimal_safe: Trial division to sqrt(N) or Sieve precomputation.

large_adversarial.name: highly_composite_numbers
large_adversarial.construction_algorithm:
# Highly composite: 720720 has 240 divisors
# Use values = [720720] * Q to stress divisor counting
values = [720720] * Q
large_adversarial.why_works: Maximum divisors forces maximum iterations in naive divisor counting.
large_adversarial.optimal_safe: Sieve-based divisor precomputation handles all values up to limit in O(N log N).

edge_cases.name: n_equals_one
edge_cases.bug_caught: 1 is not prime — most primality tests fail here

edge_cases.name: n_equals_zero
edge_cases.bug_caught: factorial(0)=1, 0^0 edge case

edge_cases.name: n_equals_two
edge_cases.bug_caught: smallest prime — sqrt(2) < 2, trial division loop may not run

edge_cases.name: large_perfect_square
edge_cases.bug_caught: sqrt precision — use int(math.isqrt(N)) not int(math.sqrt(N))

O(sqrt(n)).reasoning: sqrt(10^9) ≈ 31623. T=100 queries: 3.16M ops total → fast.

O(n log log n).reasoning: Sieve of Eratosthenes up to 10^6 runs in ~0.1s

suboptimal_algorithms.name: Brute force checking all combinations
suboptimal_algorithms.how_common: students who don't identify the greedy structure

suboptimal_algorithms.name: Wrong greedy — local optimum ≠ global optimum
suboptimal_algorithms.complexity: O(n log n) but wrong answer
suboptimal_algorithms.how_common: students who apply greedy without proof

large_adversarial.name: counter_example_for_naive_greedy
large_adversarial.kills: wrong greedy that picks largest first or smallest first
large_adversarial.construction_algorithm:
# For activity selection: intervals where largest-interval greedy fails
# Short interval at time [0,1], long interval [0,10], short at [9,10]
# Greedy by length picks [0,10], optimal picks [0,1] and [9,10]
# Scale this pattern to N intervals
large_adversarial.why_works: Naive greedy makes locally optimal choice that blocks globally optimal solution.
large_adversarial.optimal_safe: Correct greedy (by end time for interval scheduling) makes provably optimal choices.

large_adversarial.name: interdependent_costs
large_adversarial.kills: greedy that ignores interaction between choices
large_adversarial.construction_algorithm:
# Generate N items where each pair has interdependent cost
# Choosing item i changes cost of item j
# Random weights within constraints but structured to defeat local greedy
import random
items = [(random.randint(1, 1000), random.randint(1, 1000)) for _ in range(N)]
large_adversarial.why_works: Forces evaluation of global dependencies that a greedy misses.
large_adversarial.optimal_safe: DP or correct greedy with proper sorting criterion handles dependencies.

edge_cases.bug_caught: loop never runs, return initial state

edge_cases.name: all_identical_weights
edge_cases.bug_caught: tie-breaking logic, stable vs unstable sort assumption

edge_cases.bug_caught: unnecessary swaps or reordering

suboptimal_algorithms.name: Backtracking without pruning
suboptimal_algorithms.how_common: students who implement recursion without constraint propagation

large_adversarial.name: no_solution_exists
large_adversarial.construction_algorithm: N = 15  # N-Queens with no early pruning path
large_adversarial.why_works: Without pruning, explores all N! permutations. With pruning, most branches cut early.
large_adversarial.optimal_safe: Pruning cuts invalid branches at depth 1-2, reducing to manageable subtrees.

edge_cases.name: minimum_n
edge_cases.construction_algorithm: N=1 for N-Queens style, trivial solution
edge_cases.bug_caught: base case return, solution found check

edge_cases.name: n_equals_2_or_3
edge_cases.construction_algorithm: N=2 or N=3 where no solution exists
edge_cases.bug_caught: handling no-solution case, returning empty vs crashing

suboptimal_algorithms.name: Linear scan O(N) per query
suboptimal_algorithms.how_common: students who don't apply binary search

large_adversarial.name: target_at_last_index
large_adversarial.construction_algorithm:
arr = list(range(1, N + 1))
target = N  # always at last index
large_adversarial.why_works: Linear scan traverses all N elements. Binary search finds in log(N) steps.
large_adversarial.optimal_safe: Binary search finds last element in ceil(log2(N)) steps.

large_adversarial.name: target_not_present
large_adversarial.kills: linear scan with no early termination on sorted array
large_adversarial.construction_algorithm:
arr = list(range(0, 2 * N, 2))  # even numbers only
target = 1  # odd — never present
large_adversarial.why_works: Full scan traversal without finding target. Binary search eliminates half on each step.
large_adversarial.optimal_safe: Binary search terminates in O(log N) even when target absent.

edge_cases.name: target_at_index_zero
edge_cases.bug_caught: off-by-one in lo boundary

edge_cases.name: single_element_found
edge_cases.bug_caught: lo == hi base case

edge_cases.name: single_element_not_found
edge_cases.bug_caught: returning -1 vs raising exception when not found

O(log n).reasoning: log2(10^9) ≈ 30. T=100000 queries: 3M ops total → instant.

suboptimal_algorithms.name: Linear scan for each lookup O(N²)
suboptimal_algorithms.how_common: students who use list.index() or scan for membership

suboptimal_algorithms.name: Hash with poor collision handling
suboptimal_algorithms.complexity: O(N) per lookup in worst case
suboptimal_algorithms.how_common: custom hash implementations

large_adversarial.name: anti_hash_collision_sequence
large_adversarial.construction_algorithm:
MOD = 10**9 + 7
# All values are multiples of MOD → same hash bucket
values = [i * MOD for i in range(1, N + 1)]
large_adversarial.why_works: All keys hash to same bucket, degrading hash table to linked list. O(N) per lookup.
large_adversarial.optimal_safe: Python's built-in dict uses a robust hash — unaffected by this pattern.

edge_cases.name: empty_table_lookup
edge_cases.bug_caught: KeyError vs dict.get(key, default)

edge_cases.name: key_maps_to_zero
edge_cases.bug_caught: if dict[key]: treats 0 as False — use if dict.get(key) is not None

edge_cases.name: all_same_keys
edge_cases.construction_algorithm: N insertions of same key with different values
edge_cases.bug_caught: overwrite vs accumulate — depends on problem intent

suboptimal_algorithms.name: Recursion without memoization
suboptimal_algorithms.how_common: students who forget to cache results

large_adversarial.name: deep_recursion_chain
large_adversarial.construction_algorithm: N = 5000  # well above default Python limit of 1000
large_adversarial.why_works: Default Python recursion limit is 1000. Deep chain of N=5000 crashes without setrecursionlimit.
large_adversarial.optimal_safe: Iterative or tail-recursive-converted solutions unaffected.

large_adversarial.name: maximum_repeated_subproblems
large_adversarial.construction_algorithm:
# Fibonacci-style: fib(N) with N=35
# Each call branches into 2 → 2^35 calls without memo
N = 35
large_adversarial.why_works: Exponential calls without memoization. With memo: O(N) calls.
large_adversarial.optimal_safe: Memoized or bottom-up DP: O(N) time.

edge_cases.name: base_case_only
edge_cases.bug_caught: base case returns wrong value or missing return

edge_cases.name: n_equals_two
edge_cases.bug_caught: smallest non-trivial case

suboptimal_algorithms.name: O(bits) iteration when O(1) operation exists
suboptimal_algorithms.complexity: O(log N) per query when O(1) possible
suboptimal_algorithms.how_common: students who loop through bits instead of using bitwise ops directly

large_adversarial.name: all_bits_set
large_adversarial.construction_algorithm: values = [2**30 - 1] * Q  # all 30 bits set
large_adversarial.why_works: Maximum iterations for any bit-counting loop. O(1) solution (bin().count or bit_count()) unaffected.
large_adversarial.optimal_safe: bin(n).count('1') or n.bit_count() in Python 3.10+ is O(1).

large_adversarial.name: only_msb_set
large_adversarial.construction_algorithm: values = [2**29] * Q  # only most significant bit set
large_adversarial.why_works: Tests boundary of bit operations — common off-by-one in shift amount.
large_adversarial.optimal_safe: n.bit_length() - 1 gives MSB position in O(1).

edge_cases.name: n_equals_zero
edge_cases.bug_caught: bin(0)='0b0', 0.bit_length()=0 — loop never runs

edge_cases.name: n_equals_one
edge_cases.bug_caught: single bit set — base case

edge_cases.name: power_of_two
edge_cases.bug_caught: N & (N-1) == 0 iff power of 2 — common check

O(1) per query.reasoning: O(1) per query handles 10^6 queries trivially

suboptimal_algorithms.name: Nested loop O(N²)
suboptimal_algorithms.how_common: students who don't recognize two-pointer applicability

large_adversarial.name: answer_at_extremes
large_adversarial.construction_algorithm:
target = 10**9
arr = [1] * N
arr[0] = target // 2
arr[N-1] = target - target // 2
# valid pair only at positions 0 and N-1
large_adversarial.why_works: Nested loop hits valid pair only at last iteration: O(N²). Two pointers find it in first step: O(1).
large_adversarial.optimal_safe: Two pointers start at both ends by design.

edge_cases.name: no_valid_pair
edge_cases.construction_algorithm: Array where no pair satisfies condition
edge_cases.bug_caught: returning uninitialized value instead of -1

edge_cases.bug_caught: left pointer equals right pointer from start

suboptimal_algorithms.name: O(n*k) recomputing window sum from scratch
suboptimal_algorithms.how_common: students who don't maintain running sum

suboptimal_algorithms.name: Sorting inside window O(n*k*log k)
suboptimal_algorithms.how_common: students using sorted() for window min/max

large_adversarial.name: maximum_window_fluctuation
large_adversarial.construction_algorithm:
# Variable window: condition met only at very start and very end
# Forces window to expand to full N then shrink
arr = [1] * N
arr[0] = 10**9
arr[N-1] = 10**9
large_adversarial.why_works: Window expands to N and shrinks to 1, causing O(N²) work for naive recompute. O(N) for sliding approach.
large_adversarial.optimal_safe: Sliding window adds/removes one element per step. O(N) total regardless of fluctuation.

large_adversarial.name: window_never_shrinks
large_adversarial.construction_algorithm:
# All elements satisfy condition — window grows to full N
arr = [1] * N  # all equal, condition sum<=target always met
large_adversarial.why_works: Forces window to grow to maximum size without shrinking — stresses the expand path.
large_adversarial.optimal_safe: O(N) sliding window processes each element twice (add + remove) maximum.

edge_cases.name: window_larger_than_array
edge_cases.bug_caught: early return when window size exceeds array

edge_cases.bug_caught: window of size 1 — each element is its own window

suboptimal_algorithms.name: Unbalanced split O(N²)

large_adversarial.name: unbalanced_split_trigger
large_adversarial.construction_algorithm: arr = list(range(N, 0, -1))  # reverse sorted
large_adversarial.why_works: First-element pivot on reverse-sorted array always produces 0 vs N-1 split. O(N²) recursion depth.
large_adversarial.optimal_safe: Midpoint split always produces N/2 vs N/2 regardless of values.

suboptimal_algorithms.name: Union Find without path compression
suboptimal_algorithms.complexity: O(N) per find in worst case
suboptimal_algorithms.how_common: students who implement basic union without optimization

large_adversarial.name: long_union_chain
large_adversarial.why_works: Chain creates a tree of depth N-1. find(1) traverses all N nodes without path compression.
large_adversarial.optimal_safe: Path compression makes find() O(α(N)) ≈ O(1) amortized.

edge_cases.name: self_union
edge_cases.bug_caught: self-loop in parent array

edge_cases.name: already_same_component
edge_cases.construction_algorithm: union nodes already in same component
edge_cases.bug_caught: redundant union handling, cycle detection

suboptimal_algorithms.name: O(N³) brute force matrix operations
suboptimal_algorithms.how_common: students who check all cells for each query

suboptimal_algorithms.name: O(N²) scan per query when prefix sum applies
suboptimal_algorithms.how_common: students who recompute submatrix sums

large_adversarial.name: maximum_dimension_matrix
large_adversarial.construction_algorithm:
N = 1000
matrix = [[1] * N for _ in range(N)]  # 1000x1000 all ones
large_adversarial.why_works: 1000³ = 10^9 ops → TLE. O(N²) or O(N² log N) solutions handle 10^6 ops.
large_adversarial.optimal_safe: 2D prefix sum precomputes in O(N²), answers each query in O(1).

large_adversarial.name: sparse_corners_only
large_adversarial.construction_algorithm:
matrix = [[0]*N for _ in range(N)]
matrix[0][0] = 1
matrix[0][N-1] = 1
matrix[N-1][0] = 1
matrix[N-1][N-1] = 1
large_adversarial.why_works: Forces algorithms that skip zeros to handle extreme corner cases.
large_adversarial.optimal_safe: Prefix sum handles all distributions uniformly.

edge_cases.name: one_by_one_matrix
edge_cases.bug_caught: single cell — boundary conditions in all directions

edge_cases.name: one_row_matrix
edge_cases.bug_caught: row/col confusion, M=1 edge

edge_cases.name: one_col_matrix
edge_cases.bug_caught: N=1 col width — column access bounds

suboptimal_algorithms.name: Re-simulating from scratch for each query
suboptimal_algorithms.how_common: students who don't cache simulation state

suboptimal_algorithms.name: No cycle detection on periodic simulations
suboptimal_algorithms.how_common: students who simulate all steps naively

large_adversarial.name: large_step_count_with_cycle
large_adversarial.construction_algorithm:
steps = 10**18  # astronomically large
# State space is small (cycle length divides ~1000)
# Without cycle detection: TLE. With: O(cycle_length).
large_adversarial.why_works: 10^18 steps naively → never finishes. Cycle detection reduces to O(cycle_length) which is small.
large_adversarial.optimal_safe: Detect cycle with visited dict, compute remaining = steps % cycle_length.

edge_cases.name: zero_steps
edge_cases.bug_caught: initial state is the answer — loop body never executes

edge_cases.name: one_step
edge_cases.bug_caught: exactly one transition applied

edge_cases.name: step_equals_cycle_length
edge_cases.bug_caught: modular arithmetic: steps % cycle == 0 → return initial state

O(steps).reasoning: If no cycle detection required, steps ≤ 1000 to keep in O(N*steps) budget

suboptimal_algorithms.name: Exploring all game states without memoization
suboptimal_algorithms.how_common: students who simulate the game tree without caching

large_adversarial.name: high_branching_factor
large_adversarial.construction_algorithm:
# Nim with N=30 piles each of size 30
# 30^30 states without memoization
piles = [30] * 30
large_adversarial.why_works: Game tree has 30^30 nodes without memoization. XOR solution: O(N).
large_adversarial.optimal_safe: Nim-value (XOR of all piles) computed in O(N) with no search.

large_adversarial.name: all_same_pile_sizes
large_adversarial.construction_algorithm: piles = [K] * N  # all piles equal
large_adversarial.why_works: Tests that solution handles duplicate pile sizes correctly.
large_adversarial.optimal_safe: XOR of identical values: K^K^...^K. N odd → K, N even → 0.

edge_cases.name: single_pile
edge_cases.bug_caught: first player always wins with single pile > 0

edge_cases.name: all_zero_piles
edge_cases.bug_caught: XOR of all zeros = 0 → second player wins (first has no move)

edge_cases.name: one_pile_of_one
edge_cases.bug_caught: trivial case — first player takes the only stone

suboptimal_algorithms.name: DFS without cycle detection O(V+E) but crashes on cycles
suboptimal_algorithms.how_common: students who do DFS post-order without gray/white/black coloring

large_adversarial.name: maximum_length_dag
large_adversarial.construction_algorithm:
# Linear chain: 1→2→3→...→N
edges = [(i, i + 1) for i in range(1, N)]
large_adversarial.why_works: Maximum topological distance. O(V²) visit-all-predecessors approach: N² ops. Kahn's: O(V+E).
large_adversarial.optimal_safe: Kahn's algorithm processes each node and edge exactly once: O(V+E).

large_adversarial.name: cycle_at_end_of_long_path
large_adversarial.construction_algorithm:
edges = [(i, i + 1) for i in range(1, N - 1)]
edges.append((N - 1, N))  # last node
edges.append((N, N - 1))  # back edge creates cycle
large_adversarial.why_works: Cycle is only reachable after traversing N-1 edges. Early-terminating detectors miss it.
large_adversarial.optimal_safe: Kahn's algorithm: if processed nodes < N → cycle exists.

edge_cases.name: single_node_no_edges
edge_cases.bug_caught: trivial topological order: just [1]

edge_cases.name: disconnected_dag
edge_cases.bug_caught: must process all components, not just reachable from node 1

edge_cases.name: graph_with_cycle
edge_cases.construction_algorithm: edges include a back edge: (3,1) in graph 1→2→3
edge_cases.bug_caught: detect and report cycle vs crash or return wrong order

suboptimal_algorithms.name: Brute force O(N²) scan for next greater/smaller
suboptimal_algorithms.how_common: students who don't know monotonic stack trick

large_adversarial.name: full_stack_clear_at_end
large_adversarial.construction_algorithm: arr = list(range(1, N)) + [10 ** 9]
large_adversarial.why_works: Naive O(N²) scans right for each element. N-1 elements have answer at index N-1: N² total comparisons.
large_adversarial.optimal_safe: Monotonic stack processes each element at most twice (push + pop). O(N) total.

edge_cases.name: strictly_decreasing
edge_cases.bug_caught: no element is ever popped (next greater never exists for any element)

edge_cases.name: all_equal
edge_cases.bug_caught: strict vs non-strict comparison in stack condition

edge_cases.name: single_element
edge_cases.bug_caught: empty stack after processing — remaining stack elements → -1

suboptimal_algorithms.name: Recomputing sum for every query O(Q*N)
suboptimal_algorithms.how_common: students who don't precompute prefix array

large_adversarial.name: maximum_range_queries
large_adversarial.why_works: Each query recomputes sum of entire array. Q*N = 10^10 ops → TLE.
large_adversarial.optimal_safe: Prefix array answers each query in O(1). Q queries = O(Q).

edge_cases.name: query_single_element
edge_cases.bug_caught: prefix[i] - prefix[i-1] off by one

edge_cases.name: query_entire_array
edge_cases.bug_caught: prefix array boundary — prefix[N] vs prefix[N-1]

edge_cases.name: all_negative_values
edge_cases.bug_caught: prefix sum with negative values — min subarray sum logic
